title: kubectl Discovery, Contexts, Queries, Diff, Apply, and Safe Mutation · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective safe kubectl investigation and apply record; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in kubectl discovery, contexts, queries, diff, apply, and safe mutation, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in safe kubectl investigation and apply record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Trace kubeconfig loading, merged files, context, cluster, user, namespace, credentials, impersonation, and TLS server identity before a request.
Use API discovery, api-resources, api-versions, explain, raw endpoints, and versioned reference material to identify authoritative fields.
Choose get, describe, events, logs, JSONPath, custom columns, selectors, and raw JSON or YAML without parsing human tables as stable interfaces.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Kubeconfig Context Authority
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug operator is pointed at production instead of training, relies on table output, runs client dry-run, and plans an unscoped prune. Make context, namespace, server validation, field ownership, diff, and UID-safe mutation explicit. Competency probe: Trace kubeconfig loading, merged files, context, cluster, user, namespace, credentials, impersonation, and TLS server identity before a request.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-imperative-dry-run-safety-1iewf8". Tailor its kubectl-plan.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety, keep K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-imperative-dry-run-safety-1iewf8
  - competency: k8s-imperative-dry-run-safety
    artifact: kubectl-plan.yaml
    decision: imperative dry run safety
    environment: high-latency edge cluster with intermittent control-plane access
    fault-injection: drain a node while the disruption budget has no headroom
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: 1iewf8
    change: |
      context: training-cluster
      namespace: intake
      manager: learn-it-all
      preview: server-dry-run-and-diff
      mutation: server-side-apply
      delete: uid-precondition
    verify: kubectl config current-context && kubectl diff --server-side -f release.yaml
    failure: Reject implicit context, human-table parsing, client dry run as admission proof, unscoped prune, or field ownership theft.
    repair: Name cluster and namespace, use discovery and structured output, preview server behavior, scope ownership, and verify changed status.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
